items.headline: City to present draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This plan guides the city's water management strategy and drought response procedures.

items.headline: City Council reviewing 2026 community grants and event funding
items.summary: The City Council will consider applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program spring cycle on May 18, with public comment accepted. This determines which local events and neighborhood projects receive city funding.

items.headline: City develops principles for residential electrification programs
items.summary: Palo Alto is proposing principles to guide residential electrification assistance programs to help the city comply with Bay Area Air District regulations, with a status update presented to council.

items.headline: City updates housing rules for accessory dwelling units
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its municipal code to update regulations for accessory and junior accessory dwelling units in response to changes in state law and prior council direction. The changes are exempt from environmental review under California law.

items.headline: Stream corridor protection rules being reconsidered
items.summary: The City Council is reviewing an ordinance amendment to Section 18.40.140 of the municipal code regarding stream corridor protection, though the specific nature of the changes was not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: Auto dealership proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: Planning Commission will consider a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The project involves no building expansion or exterior changes, only site improvements including repaving, restriping, and landscaping.

items.headline: 195-unit apartment complex permitted near Berryessa transit station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development has received a building permit for 825 Berryessa Station Way, adding significant housing capacity near the BART station. The project represents one of the larger residential developments in recent San José permits and aligns with transit-oriented development goals.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance pilot for water customers
items.summary: San José is piloting a financial assistance program to help water customers with bills. The specifics of eligibility, benefit amounts, and timeline were not detailed in the agenda.
